performance_overview: Through 6 sessions (inception 2026-06-01) only 2 'completed trades' exist and neither is a clean signal-driven round-trip: trade 1 = the 2026-06-04 MPC long (-0.61%, stopped ~1 min after entry on a too-tight stop), trade 2 = the 2026-06-05 corrective BUY-TO-COVER of the unintended -18 MPC short created by the 06-04 EOD double-sell (+0.54%). So the 1W/1L (50%) indicator framing is state-repair noise, not edge; effective clean-signal sample is still ~0. Today (06-08) Bull was flat (+$0.00, 0.00%) while SPY closed +0.29%, so -0.29% relative on the day from holding cash. Since inception cumulative Bull +0.0067% vs SPY -2.48%: Bull is well ahead of SPY cumulatively, but entirely because it has stayed flat/cautious through a risk-off stretch, not from realized trading gains. Trend over the last 5 sessions: three straight no-trade/flat days (06-05 EOD onward), selectivity beating SPY cumulatively.
overnight_hold_insights: Still ZERO genuine overnight holds completed across all 6 sessions. The overnight_holds.closed_eod bucket reads 2 trades / 1W / 50% / avg -0.035%, but it is contaminated: both entries are intraday/EOD closes (the 06-04 MPC long close and the 06-05 corrective short-cover), neither a real carry-overnight outcome. No gap-risk data exists yet. Carry-decision policy unchanged: apply the 7 config safe-hold gates (profitable, trailing stop active, price > EMA-21, no earnings within 2 days, sentiment not negative, gap-risk cushion). Standing lesson from MPC: very low-ATR, tight-stop names that don't extend within the session are correctly closed rather than carried.
